claimText: Replenishment data is a mandatory prerequisite, in addition to the District Survey Report (DSR), for obtaining environmental clearance for sand mining.
evidenceExcerpt: Supreme Court ruled that a replenishment study is a mandatory prerequisite for environmental clearance for sand mining.
examUse: Statement-based question on the prerequisites for environmental clearance in mining projects.
distractorHooks: Replenishment data is only advisory and not mandatory.
The District Survey Report (DSR) alone is sufficient for environmental clearance without replenishment data.

claimText: The Supreme Court judgment in Deepak Kumar vs State of Haryana (2012) made environmental clearance mandatory for all minor mineral extractions, including sand.
evidenceExcerpt: Deepak Kumar vs State of Haryana (2012) made environmental clearance mandatory for all minor mineral extractions, including sand.
examUse: Match-the-following or statement-based question on landmark environmental judgments and minor minerals.
distractorHooks: Environmental clearance is only mandatory for major minerals.
The Deepak Kumar case is related to forest rights or air pollution rather than minor mineral extraction.

claimText: The EIA Notification (2016 Amendment) introduced cluster-based assessments and integrated replenishment studies into the District Survey Report (DSR).
evidenceExcerpt: EIA Notification (2016 Amendment) introduced cluster-based assessments and made replenishment studies a key part of the District Survey Report (DSR).
examUse: Statement-based question on the evolution and amendments of the EIA Notification.
distractorHooks: Cluster-based assessments were introduced in the original EIA Notification of 2006.
Replenishment studies are conducted at the state level rather than being part of the District Survey Report.

claimText: Unrestricted sand mining causes ecological damage such as riverbank erosion, lowering of groundwater tables, and increased flood risks.
evidenceExcerpt: Unrestricted sand mining causes riverbank erosion, lowers groundwater tables, and increases flood risks.
examUse: Multiple-statement question on the ecological and hydrological consequences of sand mining.
distractorHooks: Sand mining helps in raising the groundwater table.
Sand mining reduces flood risks by deepening the riverbed and increasing water carrying capacity.
